name: cpu_cad_solver
title: Engineering simulation solver
short_description: Sparse finite element solve over a fixed engineering model.

structured_text.description: Solves a bundled finite element model the way engineering simulation tools do, assembling a large sparse system and running an iterative solver to convergence across all cores. The model and tolerances are fixed so results compare directly between machines.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of solver iterations completed per second, reported as operations per second, which tracks how quickly the simulation reaches an answer.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More iterations per second means faster design cycles, since engineers wait on these solves between every change.
structured_text.reasoning: Sparse solvers stress irregular memory access and double precision math in the pattern real CAD and engineering tools use, so this reflects simulation performance far better than a clean throughput test.

domain_gaming.explanation: Game physics shares the iterative solver idea, though at far lower precision.

domain_office.explanation: Office work has no simulation component.

domain_software_development.explanation: Only developers of simulation tools see this kind of load.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Design work does not run engineering solvers.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Editing has no relationship to numerical solving.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Cloth, fluid and soft body simulation use related sparse solvers.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work does not involve finite element solving.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: This is the defining heavy workload for CAD and engineering simulation.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Sparse iterative solvers are central to a large share of scientific codes.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Some physics informed training reuses solver style kernels.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Simulation surrogates occasionally pair model inference with solver steps.

domain_data_science.explanation: Optimisation and graph methods share some sparse linear algebra.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Simulation jobs run on servers but are not a general server workload.
